verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast agree that the useful windows are poor.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY is probably one to skip—there isn’t a reliable window to justify the drive. Rey Cortiço is the least bad option after work, but the wind looks cross-on and the Surfline consistency is really lacking, so it could easily be messy. Best move is to check Surfline and MEO Beachcam before leaving and only head out if the cams look much cleaner than expected.

best.windFit: N 20 km/h · cross-onshore · poor wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 13 kJ, 5 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
